commit.message:
feat(audio-diagnostics): capture PCM audio, filter jitter outliers, improve report flow

Audio engine now captures PCM samples per transmission for debug reports.
Jitter outliers (>3x mean) are filtered from charts but kept in stats.
Worklet start threshold increased to 400ms for better P25 buffering.
Underrun counting limited to first 30ms of silence (real buffer starvation).
Report waits max 10s for active transmissions, sends multipart with raw PCM.
Stale active cards auto-cleaned after 1s of inactivity.

commit.message:
fix: add global exception handler to preserve CORS headers on 500 errors

Unhandled exceptions in FastAPI bypass CORSMiddleware, causing the browser
to see a CORS error instead of the actual server error. This handler
ensures all 500 responses are proper JSON so CORS headers are attached.

Add core-domain phases 5-13: repair, fairness, proposals, messaging, calendar, explanations, scenarios, simulation, and observations

Implements the full core-domain logic layer including:
- Repair solver and overlay management (Phase 5)
- Fairness ledger, delta tracking, and reconciliation (Phase 6)

491 tests passing across 81 test files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix auth: use getSession() instead of getUser() in Server Components

Root cause: getUser() makes a network call to Supabase API on every
request. In Server Components during RSC fetches, this call can fail
intermittently, returning null and triggering signout redirects.

- Replace getUser() with getSession() in getAuthUser() — reads JWT
  from cookies without network call (middleware already validated)
- Add try/catch in getAuthUser() for resilience
- Fix redirectWithCookies: preserve cookie options (httpOnly, secure,
  path, sameSite) when copying to redirect responses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reverse truth table row order: start from all-true to all-false

Iterates from (2^n - 1) down to 0 so rows show V,V...V first,
matching the conventional logic textbook presentation.

- Implement full e-sign flow: public signing page (/sign/[token]), sign/decline API routes, email notifications via Graph API, signature capture and storage
- Add contract line items table to signing page so signers can review what they're agreeing to
- Add AI provider abstraction layer (cloud/local LLM switching via org_settings)

- Add payroll module with periods, ancillary entries, mileage, and export
- Add overtime module with eligibility config, periods, and calculations

- Add inbox intelligence recategorise and review API routes
- Add travel estimate API for scheduling
- Multiple migration files for new features and schema extensions
- Bump version to 0.47.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(context,recovery,tui): SendBuffer for large prompts, deterministic handoffs, dedup file counts

Context rotation (rotation.go, rotation_test.go, trigger.go):
- Add SendBuffer method to PaneSpawner interface for tmux paste-buffer
  delivery, preventing truncation/corruption of large multi-line prompts
- Add paneInputSender interface and tmuxPaneInputSender for narrow
  input routing abstraction
- Add sendCompactionCommandToPane helper: routes prompts through
  SendBuffer and slash commands through SendKeys
- Add sendRotationPrompt helper: always uses SendBuffer for rotation
  handoff prompts
- Replace direct tmux.SendKeys calls in rotateAgent() and
  tryCompaction() with the new routing helpers
- trigger.go: Remove dead if/else branch (both arms were identical)
  and delegate to sendCompactionCommandToPane
- 3 new tests covering prompt→buffer, command→keys, and rotation→buffer

Recovery handoff (handoff_formatter.go, handoff_formatter_test.go):
- Add sortedMapKeys helper for deterministic map iteration
- Decisions and Findings maps now iterate in sorted key order,
  eliminating non-deterministic handoff output from Go's random
  map iteration
- New test verifying FormatHandoffContext produces identical output
  on repeated calls with same input

TUI dashboard (layout.go, layout_pure_test.go):
- Add paneKey helper returning Title if non-empty, else ID
- Rewrite fileChangesByPane to build canonical key lookup table
  mapping both Title and ID to single key, fixing double-counting
  when a change references both pane ID and title
- Replace inline file-change counting loop in BuildPaneTableRow
  with shared fileChangesByPane call
- New test verifying changes referencing both %1 and agent-a for
  same pane are counted once (3 changes from 3 records, not 6)

Swarm (auto_respawner_test.go):
- Add SendBuffer mock method to satisfy updated PaneSpawner interface

feat(routing): add 7-day lookback window for adaptive bonus computation (#1401)

Adaptive bonuses now use only recent outcomes (default 7-day window)
instead of all historical data. Falls back to full history when the
window has fewer samples than coldStartThreshold. This makes routing
responsive to recent improvements and prevents stale failure data from
dragging down correctly-routed CLIs.

commit.message:
feat: activity specialization boost in CSSL ranking

Specialty programs (hockey camp, dance academy) now rank above generalist
camps (multi-sport, traditional) when a specific activity is searched.

Ranking order: gender match → type match → is_primary tag match →
tag count (ascending) → tier → review_avg

Uses correlated subqueries included in SELECT (required by DISTINCT):
- _primary_match: 0 if searched tag has is_primary=1 for this program
- _tag_count: total program tags (1–5 = specialist, 30+ = generalist)

Only active when tag_ids are present in the search; no cost when
browsing without a specific activity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix data collection: replace dead UJN OpenData with live JN Portal API

procurement_scraper.py:
- Completely rewritten: was broken HTML scraper for dead /pozivi/lista endpoint
- Now uses JN Portal REST API with date range filter (same API as jnportal_scraper)
- Fetches contracts signed in last N days, sorted by ContractDate DESC
- Outputs recent_contracts.json + procurement_institutions.json in JN Portal schema

procurement_bulk_scraper.py:
- Updated OpenData BASE from portal.ujn.gov.rs to jnportal.ujn.gov.rs (old portal fully down)
- Individual year failures already handled gracefully (warns, continues)

graph_loader.py:
- load_procurement_data() updated to handle new array format (recent_contracts.json)
- Reuses _load_jnportal_contract/_load_jnportal_institution for consistency
- Keeps legacy single-file-per-contract support for backwards compat

detection.py (revolving_door):
- Fixed crash on empty string date: date("") raises SyntaxError in Neo4j

commit.message:
Add worktree auto-setup hook for parallel terminal sessions

SessionStart hook that detects worktree context and automatically:
- Installs dependencies (auto-detects bun/pnpm/yarn/npm from lockfile)
- Copies .env* files from main repo (skips existing)
- Copies .claude/launch.json for preview server
- Uses idempotent marker to avoid re-running

Design decision: SessionStart over WorktreeCreate to avoid replacing
default git worktree behavior. 120s timeout for dependency installs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix PAGER compatibility: ignore unknown args, fix stdin split-line bug

PAGER is less (e.g. git) don't cause an immediate exit. Also fix the
prepopulate logic so a read boundary that splits a line mid-way stores
the fragment as a partial and joins it with the next source read.

Make ghost runs universal and sync start with player

1. Ghost is now a computer-generated optimal run by default, the
   same on every device. Player's saved ghost only replaces it if
   they beat the computer's time (fewer recorded frames = faster).

2. Ghost playback only starts when the player first moves
   (timerStarted), so the ghost and player always start together
   for an accurate comparison.

endpoint responds immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ToggleGroup selected state styling via data-attribute selectors

Replace dynamic classList.add() for variant/size with data-[variant=...]
and data-[size=...] CSS selectors in static className. This ensures all
classes go through the compiler and receive the layer-components: prefix,
avoiding CSS @layer specificity conflicts where unlayered bg-transparent
overrode layered data-[state=on]:bg-accent.

Also simplify ToggleGroup Usage to outline single (S/M/L) and multiple
(Bold/Italic/Underline) examples only.

which had no starter data COPY.

Add model preview lightbox to /try page

Clicking a model card now opens a full-screen overlay showing the model
at full height alongside a card with their name, ethnicity, body build,
and height. Selection moves into the overlay via a 'Select model' button.
Closes on backdrop click, X button, or Escape key. Matches the pattern
from the Shopify app's dress-model tab.

- Wire card onClick to setPreviewModel instead of direct selection
- Add Escape key listener via useEffect

  with fade-in animation and mobile-responsive stacked layout

Update POLL S.5: live points rate to 0.1 XCN Points per XCN per day

Changed from 1:1 to 0.1 XCN Points per XCN per day for the live system:
- More sustainable rate for mainnet vs. testnet (Poll S.4 stays at 1:1)
- Testnet higher rate (1:1) specifically incentivizes testnet participation
- Live rate (0.1:1) rewards long-term staking without over-inflating points
- Transparent: 100K XCN staked = 10K XCN Points/day, always, no dilution
- Governance vote bonus: 1,000 XCN Points per vote cast

commit.message:
feat(adapters): classify signal exit codes (137/143) as transient for retry (#1401)

Exit codes 137 (SIGKILL/OOM) and 143 (external SIGTERM) now classified as
CONNECTION_ERROR instead of terminal EXECUTION_ERROR, enabling automatic retry.
Also fixes downstream test assertions for stderr error classification.
